# GRAFFITO SHORT V10

**12.31.24**

**Written by**
**mark friedberg**

***

**1. EXT. ELEMENTARY SCHOOL- LATE AFTERNOON**

Its quiet in front of this inner city school. No people. No traffic. No wind. Pigeons mingle on ledges. Until ALARM BUZZERS ERUPT. A cacophony of wings BLUR past the camera.

School DOORS FLY OPEN spewing a steady tide of knees, elbows, backpacks, and cellphones. A human tornado. The storm suddenly clears frame as fast as it filled it, returning us to the quiet empty front of the School.

Except now there stands one lone awkward figure, his face covered in curls, nose buried in a hand made sketch book. 7-year old Tony looks up, his piercing green eyes betray a deep sensitivity and wise innocence and possibly a little sadness.

**2. EXT. STREET-LATE AFTERNOON**

Tony plays with an old old defunct spray can, tossing it in the air, pretend spraying on walls. This can hasn't worked since well before Tony was born. We see an old letter M and a newer letter T hand painted on the bottom.

Tony passes four teens on a stoop practicing being cool. He pulls out his old derelict spray can and mime tags a van, trying to act cool too. They laugh at Tony, who drops his head right as his phone beeps loudly, starling him, causing him to drop then accidentally kick his phone. As it skids along the sidewalk we see MESSAGE FROM GRANDMA- He's Back

**GRANDMA (V.O.)**
> He's Back.

**TONY**
> MONK!

Tony takes off, phone in one hand, old can in the other, running as fast as he can. The Teens bewildered in the BG.

**3. EXT. TONY RUNNING ON THE STREET- MONTAGE- LATE AFTERNOON**

Feet slapping pavement. Fingers pulling over fresh graffiti. The slatted light and rumbling sounds from the subway, elevated above. Little Tony weaving though a forest of adult legs. His eyes, searching side to side. Not looking right in front. A jarring SCREECH as a gypsy cab near misses Tony filling the frame with a cacophony of sounds and images like we experienced when school let out. Tony stays still for a second, then takes off. The Camera pulls up and away showing little Tony running through his neighborhood. Camera keeps pulling up until it enters the window of a living Room.

**4. INT. GRANDMA'S APARTMENT, LIVING ROOM- LATE AFTERNOON**

The same camera, now indoors continues through an apartment. The room is filled with books, eclectic art and found objects, creating a warm outsider sensibility. FELA'S BLACK PRESIDENT beats through mismatched speakers.

27 year-old MONK has pushed the furniture aside, turning the room into an art studio. He rhythmically sways as he paints.

Colored paint flies as Tony BURSTS through the front door.

**TONY**
> DAD! YOU'RE BACK!

**MONK**
> Where I belong, Little Man!

**TONY**
> Gone so long? I missed you.

Monk stops dancing and looks directly at Tony.

**MONK**
> Trying to make a difference out there. But the mission's over and I'm home now, s’let's paint!

**TONY**
> I wanna do!

Tony pulls out his old decrepit can and mime sprays, Monk slathers a thick brush of red across his piece.

**MONK**
> Put paint whar' it ain't.

Tony joins in. They are two boys playing and grooving to AFROBEAT HORNS. Their arms trombone in and out, paint going where it wants.

**MONK (CONT'D)**
> Red! Blue!

**TONY**
> Yelloo!

**GRANDMA (V.O.)**
> TIME!

GRANDMA, looking wise and serious, marches in with two steaming bowls of rice and lentils, assesses the damage.

**GRANDMA**
> You all been busy.

**MONK**
> Little man might have to help old man out on a mission some day.

Tony brandishes his old decrepit can.

**TONY**
> Ready!

Tony's excitement turns to concern, as Grandma darkens.

**GRANDMA**
> Street's no place for either of you

**MONK**
> People see my writing on their old broke walls, makes ‘m feel better.

**GRANDMA**
> Maybe seeing YOUR name on their wall makes YOU feel better?

Monk, holding the decrepit old can, spins in place, disappears into himself. Tony, clutching his toy STUFFED ELEPHANT, disappears in his sketchbook.

**5. INT. GRANDMA'S KITCHEN- NIGHT**

Grandma and Tony finish the dishes. Monk nearby on the couch.

**TONY**
> Aren't you happy that he's home?

**GRANDMA**
> Bedtime, Tony. C'mon.

**TONY**
> Wait, but Grandma?

**GRANDMA**
> Yessss?

**TONY**
> I wanna do a mission.

**GRANDMA**
> Monk is searching out there for something he'll only find inside himself. Your mission tonight is to get to bed.

**6. INT. GRANDMA'S LIVING ROOM, SLEEPING ALCOVE- NIGHT**

A bay window converted into Tony's bedroom. The single bed fills it. Some of Tony's drawings are pinned up. Tony squeezes Elephant close, as Monk sits in, holding Tony's old decrepit paint can. He turns it over fingering the M + T.

**TONY**
> Found it under the couch. Was yours once, right?

**MONK**
> Grandma gave this to me when I was a boy bout your age. I wrote the M.

**TONY**
> I put the T! Did it spray?

**MONK**
> Never did. She said to make it work we'd have to go back to Graffito, a time when the writing was really real.

**TONY**
> When?

**MONK**
> The old, cold days when nobody had nothing. People hungry and hurting.

**TONY**
> So they wrote?

**MONK**
> Graffito Did.

**TONY**
> Graffito?

**MONK**
> Noble Citizen Outlaws led by the mythic SCRIBE.

**TONY**
> They did missions?

**MONK**
> Their Mission Statement was:
> To fight for our Freedom we paint on their Walls.

**7. EXT. GRAFFITO MONTAGE, WALL- NIGHT**

Tony's fantasy of the broken down streets in black and white. Masked Ninja-like figures RAPPEL on ropes, SPRAYING brightly colored words and images into the black and white night.

**TONY (V.O.)**
> Graffito never got caught?

**MONK (V.O)**
> Never. Cops’d show up, but they'd all of a sudden vanish, like ghosts

Same building now covered with a vividly painted mural washed in blue and red lights. No Graffito, Cops stumble around.

**8. EXT. GRAFFITO MONTAGE, ROOFTOP- NIGHT**

Graffito at work, faces covered, moving in a unified rhythm.

**MONK (V.O.)**
> Each member had a skill, but only Scribe had the Magic Spray Can.

Insert: Scribe's can looks a lot like Tony's old decrepit Can.

**TONY (V.O.)**
> What'd it do?

**MONK (V.O.)**
> It painted doors that actually opened. Let the poor in.

**9. EXT. GRAFFITO MONTAGE, STORE- NIGHT**

An orange painted door opens from outside a closed big box store as a family runs out holding bags of groceries.

**MONK (V.O.)**
> Scribe was our Robin Hood.

**10. INT. GRANDMA'S LIVING ROOM, SLEEPING ALCOVE- CONT**

**TONY**
> And Graffito the Merry Men!

**MONK**
> All right merry man, sleep time.

**TONY**
> But Dad?

**MONK**
> But yeah?

**TONY**
> Why're you not home a lot?

**MONK**
> I...uh... work issues. Sometimes I just gotta lay low.

**TONY**
> Like hiding?

Monk puts his head under a blanket. Tony joins him, giggles.

**11. INT. GRANDMA'S LIVING ROOM- LATER THAT NIGHT**

Tony wakes up, walks over to Monk's couch and Looks for him inside the salad of blankets.

**TONY**
> Monk?...you still hiding?

But Monk's gone. Tony's expression drops.

**12. EXT. STREET AROUND THE CORNER- NIGHT**

Monk spinning under a streetlight, sadly humming Thelonius Monk's MYSTERIOSO. Tony joins his dad and they spin together.

**TONY**
> Hey, Monk... Dad? You in there?

**MONK**
> Let's get you home, little man.

**TONY**
> We do a mission then I get you home. You ready? C'mon!

He picks up his dad's art bag and runs off.

**13. EXT. ELEMENTARY SCHOOL- NIGHT**

Monk dances as he paints a sky onto the side of the School. He's a virtuoso, utterly consumed.

Tony picks up a brush and emphatically tries to paint mountains onto the mural. They look shaky.

**TONY**
> How'dya make yours look real?

**MONK**
> Becoming my animal self, let's the paint come alive.

Tony, inspired, paints a dragon. It comes out twisted, though there's something beautiful and original about it.

**TONY**
> Wish I could paint like you.

Monk stops what he's doing, inspired by Tony's work.

**MONK**
> You paint straight from your heart. that's really real, Little Man.

Tony tries his old decrepit can, nothing happens. Monk puts his hands over Tony's. They hold the can together, swaying to an imagined beat.

**MONK (CONT'D)**
> Give your mind to your body,

**TONY AND MONK (PRACTICED ROUTINE)**
> And let it flow.

SIREN SQUAWKS. They're consumed in SPINNING LIGHTS. Tony is terrified. Note: we don't see Cops, only blinding lights.

**COPS (V.O.)**
> Freeze! Now!

Tony still has the Old SPRAY CAN in his hand.

Monk spins fast. He's not violent, but not in control either. Cop lights bear down. Tony, Can in hand, not sure what to do.

**MONK**
> Use it! Make a door!!

**TONY**
> It doesn't work?

**MONK**
> Move your body!

Monk's body's SLAMMED on the squad car. Lights close on Tony.

**TONY (TO HIMSELF)**
> Let it.....

Tony tries the SPRAY CAN, but it sputters. Lights closing.

**MONK/TONY**
> Flow!

Tony spins in place, starts to feel a distant beat. The rhythm gets louder until we hear Miriam Makeba’s PATA PATA. Tony is scared but he is also dancing now. As he sways, a CLOUD OF ORANGE emerges from the Can and engulfs Tony in an animatic burst of still images- relating stylistically to the school dismissal and the gypsy cab near miss. This seemingly spectral reaction to over stimulus is related to Tony's unique ability to work the Magic Can. The images flash and ultimately, congeal into an ORANGE DOOR.

**MONK**
> GO!

Tony jumps into the doorway. Monk is now bent over the cop car. As Tony pulls the orange door closed, Monk's amazed face, washed in police light, is the last thing he sees.

**14. SPACE**

Blackness. Tony floats by like an untethered astronaut, too amazed to be scared, the SPRAY CAN still in his hand, spewing a cacophony of images that register into stars and nebulae.

**TONY (V.O.)**
> Monk? Grandma....?

He sprays a green cloud whose fragments congeal into an elevated subway staircase. He steps on and descends.

**15. EXT. IMAGINARY STREET- NIGHT**

Tony steps off onto an ever forming street. It looks like his neighborhood, but with a painted sky and distant scraggly mountains. Live animals walk the streets instead of people.

A terrifying SCREECH. Out of a distant fart of black smoke from a crooked castle on the horizon emerges the giant, sideways flying and generally messed up dragon, MOTHERLOAD.

**TONY**
> What the..?

She flies at Tony and dives, the craziness of her wings remind us of the pigeons at school. Tony sprays out a cloud, which forms into a hot dog cart umbrella, deflecting the Dragon and her fire.

**MOTHERLOAD**
> Good to see you again, old flame.

She dives again. Tony holds out the can to spray her, but Motherload grabs it with a gnarled talon. She tries to brake, but bounces into a building making fire jet into the air.

**TONY**
> I need my can back.

**MOTHERLOAD**
> What makes you think it's yours?

**TONY**
> It's Monk's, but he gave it to me so I could save myself and now that I'm here I need to get back so I can save Monk.

**MOTHERLOAD**
> It's late. Time to get to bed.

Motherload takes off flying crookedly towards the Mountains, the SPRAY CAN leaking wisps of color, which turn into falling bread crumbs trailing behind the corkscrewing Dragon.

**TONY**
> Huh? Wait! Don't...

ELEPHANT gently sidles up, Tony does a double take at his now life sized toy Elephant, which also somehow, can talk to him.

**ELEPHANT**
> She's been waiting a long time.

**TONY**
> But I...just got here?

**ELEPHANT**
> You sure?

A distant screech from the other side of the Mountains.

**TONY**
> To get back, I need that Magic Can.

**ELEPHANT**
> Sounds like a plan.

**TONY**
> It does? How we gonna find a Dragon hiding in the mountains at night?

Elephant eats a bread crumb. Takes a few steps, eats another. Tony's face brightens. Off they go.

**16. EXT. BOTTOM OF CASTLE WALL- MORNING**

Elephant eats the last crumb as they emerge from the woods.

They look up and see an old craggily castle. ML perched high on a wall, playing with the CAN. It won't work for her.

**MOTHERLOAD**
> You broke it.

**TONY**
> (Whispers to Elephant)
> We get her down, then I grab it.

ML hurls a fireball at them, near miss.

**ELEPHANT**
> You might have to go up and get it?

Tony starts to levitate. This confuses him until he realizes that Elephant's lifting him with her trunk.

**ELEPHANT (CONT'D)**
> She likes to dance, btw.

**17. EXT. TOP OF CASTLE WALL- MORNING**

Tony side-steps his way along the broken wall. ML points the CAN at him, but it doesn't SPRAY.

**TONY**
> I couldn't make it work either.

**MOTHERLOAD**
> And then?

**TONY**
> Monk told me to free my animal.

**MOTHERLOAD**
> Your animal?

**TONY**
> He's helping me to paint better so I can make a difference.

**MOTHERLOAD**
> The only difference he makes is whenever he writes the COPS come.

**TONY**
> I want to make a difference, but I think I'm just different.

**MOTHERLOAD**
> Good.

**TONY**
> I'm always drawing cause it's the only way I can stay calm.

**MOTHERLOAD**
> Calmness is not what I am good at.

**TONY**
> And no matter how hard I try, everything comes out crooked.

**MOTHERLOAD**
> I happen to like crooked.

**TONY**
> I don't know whats wrong with me?

**MOTHERLOAD**
> Maybe what is wrong with you is exactly what's right with you? You're the only one who can make the Magic Can work.

ML and Tony share a meaningful moment, just looking at each other. Then she tries the can, breaking the spell and squirting a paint puff onto her face. She coughs.

**TONY**
> Gimme it.

She does not. Tony starts to spin in place, very Monk-like.

**MOTHERLOAD**
> What are you doing?

Tony sways, to an unheard beat.

**TONY**
> Flowing.

After some flowing, we also HEAR the music, its Pata Pata. ML SWOOPS in next to Tony and they flow together.

**TONY (CONT'D)**
> Close your eyes. Free your animal.

They move together. Tony takes the can, emitting rainbows of intensifying color. Mother Load dances freely with wings out-stretched and then...accidentally knocks Tony off the wall.

**TONY (CONT'D)**
> Ahhhhhhh.

**MOTHERLOAD**
> Tony!

She launches herself towards Tony, who's falling fast.

**ELEPHANT**
> Tony, the Can!

Tony sprays out an image cloud. A rope forms. He grabs it and starts rappelling down, like in the Graffito Story.

ML's gnarled talon GRABS the rope, jerking it to a stop.

**MOTHERLOAD**
> Answer one question.

Tony is scared and frustrated and trapped. And curious.

**MOTHERLOAD (CONT'D)**
> What is your real name?

Tony looks long into her (Grandma's) Eyes. Beat.

**TONY**
> Is it Scribe?

**MOTHERLOAD**
> Welcome back.

**18. EXT. ELEMENTARY SCHOOL- NIGHT**

Cop lights spinning, the Orange Door Tony painted in Monk's sky mural opens. Monk, still racked over the car is right where we left him. Tony steps out and points the can at COPS.

**TONY**
> Freeze!

He pulls his amazed father into the Doorway. They hug.

**TONY (CONT'D)**
> Dad, you ready?

Camera stays on the sky mural as the door closes. The Orange Door vanishes as a painted star shoots across the celestial sky. Happy screeches fade on the other side of the door.

Pata Pata Plays.

The End.
